name: Heavy-duty gas turbines
severityNote: Lead times 4-5 years; OEM books full to 2030
description: H-class and J-class heavy-duty gas turbines are the fastest path to firm dispatchable generation for behind-the-meter datacenter power. The three-OEM oligopoly (GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi Power) is sold out through end-of-decade for both new builds and major-overhaul slots.
stackPosition: Nickel-base superalloy + investment castings (PCC, Doncasters) → single-crystal turbine blades → hot-section assembly → compressor and combustor build → full unit test at OEM facility → ship by rail or barge → site erection and commissioning → grid sync OR direct datacenter feed. The OEM hot-section is the visible chokepoint; single-crystal blade casting is the upstream constraint.

supplyStructure.name: Other (Ansaldo, Solar Turbines)
supplyStructure.note: Smaller frames; not at datacenter scale

indicators.name: GE Vernova Gas Power orders, backlog, and book-to-bill
Siemens Energy Gas Services orders + services attach commentary
Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Energy segment results
DOE / state PUC interconnection rulings on co-located gas + datacenter
McCoy Power Reports gas turbine market data

companies.name: GE Vernova
companies.note: Pure-play public OEM with US-listed liquidity; cleanest expression

companies.name: Siemens Energy
companies.note: Gas Services + Grid combined; European listing

companies.name: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries
companies.note: Mitsubishi Power parent; conglomerate discount applies

companies.name: Precision Castparts (Berkshire)
companies.note: Hot-section investment castings — unlisted since BRK acquisition

companies.name: Wartsila
companies.note: Reciprocating gas engines — smaller-scale alternative for fast-start peakers

insight: Heavy-duty gas turbines are the cleanest behind-the-meter generation play available, and the OEM oligopoly is so tight that pricing has crossed from cost-plus to scarcity-rent within 18 months. GEV is the cleanest US-listed expression and has already moved hard; the second-order trade is hot-section materials — single-crystal blade casting at PCC and Doncasters is an even narrower oligopoly with stronger pricing power but no listed pure play. The under-watched leg is reciprocating engines (Wartsila, Cummins) for sub-100 MW colos where waiting 5 years for a frame turbine kills the project IRR.

debates.question: Do hyperscaler behind-the-meter gas builds reach 10 GW of announced capacity by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: On track — Meta, Microsoft, and AWS have collectively signaled >6 GW already

debates.question: Does a major hyperscaler walk back gas commitments under sustainability pressure by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Unlikely while load growth dominates the agenda; carbon offsets absorb the optics
